content.text: Contoso Retail requires all workplace incidents, injuries, illnesses, and near-misses to be reported promptly and accurately. Reporting enables us to provide proper medical care, fulfill legal obligations, and most importantly — identify root causes so we can prevent future incidents. This policy applies to all full-time, part-time, temporary, and contract workers on Contoso Retail premises.
Report ALL of the following events, regardless of whether an injury or damage occurred:

items: Any injury or illness that occurs in the workplace or while performing work-related activities.
Near-misses: An unplanned event that did not result in injury or damage, but could have (e.g., a forklift stops short of striking a pedestrian, a load falls from racking but no one is nearby).
Property damage: Damage to equipment, racking, vehicles, or the building caused by workplace activities.
Security incidents: Theft, unauthorized access, or threatening behavior.
Environmental releases: Chemical spills, fluid leaks that enter drains or the environment.

content.text: There is no minimum threshold for reporting. When in doubt, report it. Near-miss reporting is especially valued — team members who report near-misses are recognized, not penalized.
All incidents must be reported within specific timeframes:

rows: Any injury requiring first aid or beyond
Amputation, loss of eye, inpatient hospitalization

content.text: State-specific reporting timelines for workers' compensation may be shorter than the above. Refer to your state's Workers' Compensation document for state-specific deadlines.

sections.heading: How to File an Incident Report

items: Notify your supervisor verbally as soon as the incident occurs.
Seek medical attention if needed. For life-threatening injuries, call 911 first.
Preserve the incident scene — do not move equipment or clean up (unless preserving the scene poses an ongoing safety hazard) until the supervisor and Safety Coordinator have assessed it.
Complete the paper Incident Report form (available at the supervisor station and Zone F safety board) with your supervisor within your shift.
Alternatively, use the Warehouse Assistant agent to log a safety issue — the agent will collect all required fields and submit to the Safety Issue Log.
The supervisor reviews the report for completeness and submits to the Safety Coordinator before end of shift.
The Safety Coordinator determines if the incident is OSHA recordable and enters it into the OSHA 300 log if required.

content.text: When an incident is reported to a supervisor, they must:

items: Ensure the injured worker receives appropriate medical care before any paperwork.
Secure the incident scene and investigate root cause within 24 hours.
Complete the Supervisor's Incident Investigation Report (SIIR) in addition to the employee's report.
Identify at least 3 contributing factors and recommend corrective actions.
Brief the incoming shift supervisor during the next handoff.
Follow up with the injured worker within 48 hours regarding their condition and return-to-work status.
Track corrective actions to completion — do not close an incident until all corrective actions are implemented.

content.text: A work-related injury or illness is OSHA recordable if it results in any of the following:

items: Days away from work.
Restricted work or job transfer.
Medical treatment beyond first aid.
Loss of consciousness.
Diagnosis of a significant injury or illness by a healthcare professional.
Needlestick or sharps injury involving blood or other potentially infectious material.

content.text: First aid does NOT make an incident recordable. First aid examples: non-prescription medication at non-prescription strength, tetanus immunization, cleaning/flushing/soaking surface wounds, use of non-rigid means of support (bandages, elastic wrap), over-the-counter eye patches.
OSHA 300 logs are maintained by the Safety Coordinator and posted annually (February 1 – April 30) in the break room as required by law.

Contoso Retail strictly prohibits any form of retaliation against team members who report injuries, illnesses, near-misses, or safety concerns. This includes but is not limited to: termination, demotion, reassignment, or disciplinary action taken in response to a safety report. Any suspected retaliation should be reported immediately to HR or the Warehouse Manager.
